sourceMovie.title: Midnight Express
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Midnight Express works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Shawshank Redemption
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Midnight Express clicked for you, The Shawshank Redemption (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Midnight Express?
faq.answer: Try The Shawshank Redemption, Blow, Cape Fear—each shares DNA with Midnight Express while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Midnight Express?
faq.answer: Midnight Express (1978) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Midnight Express worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
